question: The 8-state Potts model on a two-dimensional square lattice is studied with a layered aperiodic modulation of nearest-neighbor couplings along one direction. The couplings take two values K0=K and K1=K*r (with coupling ratio r=5), arranged according to the paper-folding (PF) aperiodic sequence (generated by pair substitutions 11->1101, 10->1100, 01->1001, 00->1000 with last digit omitted), which has logarithmically divergent fluctuations (wandering exponent omega_PF=0). Without the aperiodic modulation, the 8-state Potts model has a FIRST-ORDER transition. Swendsen-Wang Monte Carlo finite-size scaling is used at the self-dual critical coupling. What is the ORDER of the phase transition of the 8-state Potts model with the paper-folding layered aperiodic sequence? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: 8-state Potts model on 2D square lattice with paper-folding layered aperiodic coupling sequence
metadata.source_excerpt: For the 8-state Potts model with the paper-folding (PF) layered sequence... Monte Carlo finite-size scaling at the self-dual critical coupling... shows behavior consistent with a second-order (continuous) transition, indicating that paper-folding aperiodicity induces a change from first-order to second-order
metadata.difficulty_note: Aperiodic perturbation with wandering exponent omega=0 (logarithmically divergent fluctuations) is marginally relevant and induces an order change from first to second order; Thue-Morse (omega=-inf) is irrelevant and leaves first-order intact.
